# 🌱 use-agy

> A tiny **Bun + Effect v4** workspace for source-driven agent skills and GitHub Actions — powered by **skill-gen** and **workflow-gen**.

  <img alt="Runtime: Bun" src="https://img.shields.io/badge/runtime-Bun%201.3-black?logo=bun&logoColor=white">

---

## ✨ What is this?

`use-agy` is a small monorepo for building and maintaining **agent skills** and generated CI workflows. Instead of hand-editing derived files, you embed fragments right next to the code they describe and let the local generators keep the published artifacts in sync.

It's intentionally lean: no build step, no bundler, no global installs. Bun runs the TypeScript directly, Effect v4 powers the CLI, and everything is gated behind a single `bun run ci`.

## 🎁 Highlights

- **`skill-gen`** — scans source for `//<skill-gen>` markers, merges fragments by skill name, and writes `.agents/skills/<name>/SKILL.md`. Includes a drift `check` for CI and an `e2e-test` against fixtures.
- **`workflow-gen`** — scans source for `//<workflow-gen>` markers, deep-merges YAML fragments by workflow name, and writes `.github/workflows/<name>.yml` plus `required-workflows.json`.
- **Effect v4 (`effect-smol`)** — the CLI is built on `effect/unstable/cli`; the v4 source is vendored as a submodule for signature-level reference.
- **Curated skills & commands** — a hand-tuned set of `domain-*`, `workflow-*`, `meta-*`, and `practice-*` skills plus slash-commands under `.agents/`.
- **Batteries-included toolchain** — Biome, knip, Vitest (`@effect/vitest`), and shared tsconfig presets, wired into one CI gate.

## 📦 Layout

```
.agents/
  skills/      Agent skills (hand-written + generated by skill-gen)
  commands/    Slash-command definitions
.github/
  actions/     Shared GitHub Actions setup
  workflows/   Generated by workflow-gen
packages/
  ci/actions/                 Co-located CI checkers
  ci/skill-gen/               Skill-file generator (Bun + Effect v4 CLI)
  ci/workflow-gen/            GitHub Actions workflow generator
  tooling/typescript-config/  Shared tsconfig presets (base.json, bun.json)
plans/                        Design & implementation plans
vendor/
  effect-smol/                Effect v4 source — git submodule, reference only
```

## 🚀 Getting started

This repo vendors the Effect v4 source as a submodule, so clone recursively:

```bash
git clone --recurse-submodules <repo-url>
cd use-agy
bun install
```

Already cloned without submodules? Run `git submodule update --init`.

## 🛠️ skill-gen

Embed a fragment anywhere in your source:

```ts
//<skill-gen>
// ---
// name: my-skill
// description: "What this skill teaches agents."
// ---
//
// ## Section Heading
//
// Markdown body content here.
//</skill-gen>
```

Then generate and verify:

```bash
bun run skill-gen:generate   # write .agents/skills/<name>/SKILL.md
bun run skill-gen:check      # CI drift check (exits non-zero if stale)
bun run skill-gen:e2e-test   # run against fixtures
```

See [`packages/ci/skill-gen/SPEC.md`](packages/ci/skill-gen/SPEC.md) for the full specification.

## 🧵 workflow-gen

Embed a GitHub Actions fragment anywhere in source:

```ts
//<workflow-gen>
// name: ci
// required: true
// on:
//   push:
//     branches: [main]
//   pull_request: {}
// jobs:
//   lint:
//     name: Lint
//     steps:
//       - uses: actions/checkout@v5
//       - uses: ./.github/actions/setup
//       - run: bun run check
//</workflow-gen>
```

Then generate and verify:

```bash
bun run workflow-gen:generate   # write .github/workflows/<name>.yml
bun run workflow-gen:check      # CI drift check (exits non-zero if stale)
bun run workflow-gen:e2e-test   # run against fixtures
```

See [`packages/ci/workflow-gen/SPEC.md`](packages/ci/workflow-gen/SPEC.md) for the full specification.

## ✅ Development

```bash
bun run ci          # biome + typecheck + vitest + knip + generator drift checks
bun run check       # Biome (format + lint)
bun run typecheck   # tsc --noEmit, per package
bun run test        # Vitest
bun run knip        # unused-code analysis
```

Conventions for agents and contributors live in [`AGENTS.md`](AGENTS.md) (symlinked as `CLAUDE.md`) and the skills under `.agents/skills/`.
